cards.text: What are the four actions of pectoralis major?
cards.answer: Adduction, medial rotation, flexion, and extension from flexed position

tip.tip: A powerful mover of the shoulder joint

cards.text: What fascia lies between pectoralis major and minor, and what pierces it?
cards.answer: Clavipectoral fascia; pierced by cephalic vein, thoracoacromial artery, lateral pectoral nerve, and lymphatics

tip.tip: Named for its attachment between clavicle and pectoralis minor

cards.text: What is the extent of the breast?
cards.answer: Vertically from rib 2 to rib 6; horizontally from lateral border of sternum to mid-axillary line

cards.text: What is the axillary tail of Spence?
cards.answer: Extension of breast tissue into the axilla through an opening in the deep fascia

tip.tip: Important site for breast lumps that may be missed

cards.text: What percentage of breast lymph drains to axillary nodes?

tip.tip: The most common route of breast cancer metastasis

cards.text: Which ligaments support the breast and can cause skin dimpling when invaded by cancer?

tip.tip: Fibrous septa running from deep fascia to skin

cards.text: What is the nerve supply of pectoralis minor?

tip.tip: Despite its name, the medial pectoral nerve passes through pectoralis minor

cards.text: What is the retromammary space and its clinical significance?
cards.answer: A loose areolar tissue space between the breast and pectoral fascia; allows breast mobility and is used in surgical dissection

tip.tip: Cancer invading this space fixes the breast to the chest wall

cards.text: What congenital anomaly involves absence of the breast?

tip.tip: Athelia refers to absence of just the nipple

cards.text: Name the origin of pectoralis minor.

tip.tip: A thin triangular muscle deep to pectoralis major
